heading: Definitions
label: 98h–3 Definitions
notes: Amendments2023—Par. (3). added par. (3) and struck out former par. (3) which read as follows: “The term ‘national technology and industrial base’ has the meaning given such term in .”Pub. L. 118–31section 2500 of title 102021—Par. (3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 117–81
amendments: Amendments2023—Par. (3). added par. (3) and struck out former par. (3) which read as follows: “The term ‘national technology and industrial base’ has the meaning given such term in .”Pub. L. 118–31section 2500 of title 102021—Par. (3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 117–81

children.text: The term “strategic and critical materials” means materials that (A) would be needed to supply the military, industrial, and essential civilian needs of the United States during a national emergency, and (B) are not found or produced in the United States in sufficient quantities to meet such need.
The term “national emergency” means a general declaration of emergency with respect to the national defense made by the President or by the Congress.

children.label: A
children.text: the United States or any territory or possession of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: a country of the national technology and industrial base, as such term is defined in ; orsection 4801 of title 10

children.label: C
children.text: a qualifying country, as defined in section 225.003 of the Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement or any successor document.
